CVE-2021-3712.168dca2f7036723344157abde7add4833cb9b920:
Revise GENERAL_NAME_to_jobject. (#1032)

This fixes a few issues. First, the comment is a little confusing. The
value is an IA5String. The issue isn't whether it might accidentally be
a T61String, but that its contents may be invalid for an IA5String.

Next, I've replaced ASN1_PRINTABLE_type with a loop. ASN1_PRINTABLE_type
does actually work, but it's normally used in OpenSSL to auto-detect a
string type for a Latin-1 string, which is a little confusing.

Finally, I've tweaked this so it does not rely on the ASN1_STRING being
NUL-terminated. The ASN1_STRING is almost always NUL-terminated, but
best not to rely on this. (See CVE-2021-3712.)

CVE-2017-13309.e56958e7dea05c1784317f139e2216e2e707d391:
Fix SSLEngine bug with multiple heap buffer inputs. (#485)

When the SSLEngine overload that accepts an array of ByteBuffers is
called with heap buffers for both the source and destination, those
heap buffers are converted to direct buffers for passing to JNI by way
of copying them to a single temporary direct buffer.  A bug in the
reading of the encrypted data out of BoringSSL resulted in the data
being placed at the wrong offset of the temporary buffer, meaning that
the output data was prefixed in the worst case by the plaintext.

Fixes CVE-2017-13309.
